subject: [RFC PATCH 33/45] mm: page_alloc: refuse to taint clean SPBs for atomic NORETRY callers

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch modifies the page allocation logic in Linux to prevent clean superpageblocks from being tainted when an atomic allocation request is made without retrying. The patch targets callers that pass __GFP_NORETRY, which indicates they have a fallback path for failure. In such cases, tainting a clean superpageblock would incur a lasting cost and block future allocations. The patch skips the relaxation steps for NORETRY callers and returns NULL immediately to allow their fallback paths to handle the failure.
